APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS VIA COURTCALL. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 3/25/24 IS CONTINUED TO 6/24/24 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL DISCOVERY TRACKS THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PLAINTIFF TO DOWNLOAD THE ORDER AND SERVE IT ON ALL PARTIES. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

JOSEPH BRAVO'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN THE APPROPRIATE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Atty. PETER FINN, Esq. of J Supple Law (tel: 415-366-5533) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF LUDMILA LOZINSKAYA's Motion To File Under Seal.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ludmila Lozinskaya's unopposed sealing motion is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOE BRAVO, Esq. of Bravo Law Offices (tel: 415-512-6700) appearing with Plaintiff Ludmila Lozinskaya.
Atty. PETER FINN, Esq. of J Supple Law (tel: 415-366-5533) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel as to PLAINTIFF LUDMILA LOZINSKAYA.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Joseph Bravo's motion to be relieved as counsel is granted. The attorney-client relationship is irreparably broken. Only the presiding judge (Department 206) may continue trials, but that is Department 302's recommendation here.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order in the appropriate judicial council form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
